headline: Domestic Gas Price Subsidy Creates RM6B Arbitrage to Petrochemicals
context: Malaysia's subsidised domestic gas price at RM28.36 per mmBTU versus market price of RM48 per mmBTU creates a RM6 billion annual subsidy that disproportionately benefits petrochemical companies rather than households.

cards.big: RM6 billion gas subsidy — 78% goes to petrochemical factories and power plants, not to Malaysian households
cards.sub: Top 5 industrial gas consumers receive RM3.2 billion in subsidised gas — more than entire affordable housing budget

cards.big: Subsidised gas at RM28.36 versus market RM48 per mmBTU means Malaysia sells domestic gas at 41% discount to own industry
cards.sub: Petronas foregoes RM6 billion annually in potential gas revenue — effectively subsidising private sector chemical companies

cards.big: Household piped gas users number only 280,000 — receiving RM120 million in subsidy versus industry's RM5.8 billion
cards.sub: Industrial gas subsidy per factory averages RM2.4 million annually — dwarfing the RM430 per household

cards.big: Gas subsidy has no sunset clause or efficiency requirement — companies receive cheap gas regardless of productivity or export contribution
cards.sub: Energy Commission reviews gas tariffs every 6 months but industrial subsidy quantum is set by cabinet — not by economic criteria

cards.big: Calling it an energy subsidy obscures the truth — it's a RM6 billion annual transfer from national resources to private industry
Should industrial gas subsidies be phased to market rates over 5 years with RM2 billion redirected to household energy assistance?
